2603.29913.title: SISA: A Scale-In Systolic Array for GEMM Acceleration
2603.29913.paper_summary: The currently dominant AI/ML workloads, such as Large Language Models (LLMs), rely on the efficient execution of General Matrix-Matrix Multiplication (GEMM) operations. Thus, most systems are equipped with dedicated matrix hardware accelerators based on square Systolic Arrays (SAs) of Processing Elements (PEs). While this organization was effective for traditional Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), LLMs introduce input-dependent and highly skewed matrices, leading to underutilized SA resources. To address this challenge, we propose SISA (Scale-In Systolic Array), a novel SA architecture that partitions the traditional square array into horizontal rectangular slabs. With minimal overhead, SISA exposes parallelism through independently scheduled slabs for efficient execution of small or skewed matrix shapes, while retaining full-array operation for large GEMMs. SISA achieves up to 8.52x speedup and 93% energy-delay-product (EDP) reduction for representative LLMs compared to a state-of-the-art monolithic SA with the same number of PEs.
2603.29913.paper_summary_zh: 目前主導的 AI/ML 工作負載，如大型語言模型 (LLMs)，依賴於通用矩陣-矩陣乘法 (GEMM) 操作的高效執行。<lb/>因此，大多數系統配備了基於處理單元 (PEs) 的方形脈動陣列 (SAs) 的專用矩陣硬體加速器。<lb/>雖然這種組織對於傳統深度神經網絡 (DNNs) 是有效的，但 LLMs 引入了依賴於輸入且高度偏斜的矩陣，導致 SA 資源未被充分利用。<lb/>為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了 SISA（縮放進脈動陣列），這是一種新穎的 SA 架構，將傳統的方形陣列劃分為水平矩形板塊。<lb/>在最小的開銷下，SISA 通過獨立調度的板塊暴露了並行性，以高效執行小型或偏斜的矩陣形狀，同時保留對於大型 GEMM 的全陣列操作。<lb/>與擁有相同數量 PEs 的最先進單體 SA 相比，SISA 對於代表性的 LLMs 實現了高達 8.52 倍的加速和 93% 的能量延遲乘積 (EDP) 減少。
2603.29913.author: Luigi Altamura et.al.
2603.29913.authors: Luigi Altamura, Alessio Cicero, Mateo Vázquez Maceiras, Mohammad Ali Maleki, Pedro Trancoso
